vulnerabilities.description: JWT implementation missing explicit algorithm validation - vulnerable to algorithm confusion attacks (none/HS256 bypass)
vulnerabilities.fix: Add explicit algorithm validation: jwt.WithValidMethods([]string{"HS256"}) in token parsing

vulnerabilities.description: No minimum secret key length validation - weak secrets allowed
vulnerabilities.fix: Add validation in DefaultConfig: if len(secretKey) < 32 { return error }

vulnerabilities.description: SAML signature validation logic not visible in snippet - incomplete code
vulnerabilities.fix: Ensure full signature validation with IdP certificate and XML wrapping attack prevention

vulnerabilities.description: AuthMiddleware uses prefix-based path skipping - could allow /api/v1/private to match /api/v1
vulnerabilities.fix: Use exact path matching or suffix-based exclusion with proper path normalization

vulnerabilities.description: No visible PKCE implementation for native/mobile OAuth flows
vulnerabilities.fix: Add PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) for non-confidential clients

vulnerabilities.description: Refresh tokens valid for 7 days may be too long for sensitive applications
vulnerabilities.fix: Consider reducing to 24-48 hours or implementing refresh token rotation

vulnerabilities.description: State parameter uses crypto/rand but nonce expiration not visible
vulnerabilities.fix: Add TTL for state nonces (5-10 minutes) and cleanup routine

owasp_checks.detail: Auth middleware present with JWT validation
Argon2id with OWASP parameters, crypto/rand used
Parameterized queries implied, validation middleware present
Default timeouts and params are secure
Using golang-jwt/jwt v5, x/oauth2 - current versions
State validation present but JWT algo check missing

critical_fixes: Add explicit JWT algorithm validation: jwt.WithValidMethods([]string{"HS256"}) in token parsing to prevent algorithm confusion attacks
Add minimum secret key length validation (32+ characters) in DefaultConfig
